subject: FW: FOR je'S ATTENTION - CORRESPONDENCE WITH WEIZMANN INSTITUTE - 2
body:
Lesley Groff
Executive Assistant to Jeffrey Epstein
 
 

________________________________

From: egneurocog@aol.com [mailto:egneurocog@aol.com] 
Sent: Monday, March 24, 2008 12:41 PM
To: Lesley Groff; Lesley Groff
Subject: FOR je'S ATTENTION - CORRESPONDENCE WITH WEIZMANN INSTITUTE - 2



-----Original Message-----
From: Misha Tsodyks <misha@weizmann.ac.il>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Sent: Mon, 24 Mar 2008 11:45 am
Subject: Re: Fwd: RESEARCH PROPOSAL


Dear Elkhonon, 
 
Please see attached a sketch of the possible project, very brief and
informal of course. As you see, it is heavily skewed to my own research,
but I hope you will see how we can integrate it with your ideas. 
 
Regarding the possible logistics, I think there are two basic options if
this is to be based at Weizmann. One is what we talked about on the
phone, namely a grant to two of us for a collaborative research, which
would allow us to have a small group of students/postdocs at Weizmann
and some funds for travel etc. This would be a most straightforward and
relatively modest investment, say 100-200K per year for 2-3 years. The
more ambitious thing to do would be to set up a comp. neuroscience
center at Weizmann, with me as a director and you as an external
co-director/adviser. We are talking now about few million USDs as an
endownment. The advantage would be that we could use some of the money
to fund theoretically-motivated experiments in one of the labs here or
beyond. For example, we have cutting-edge labs doing FMRI and optical
imaging that could do experiments for us. The issue is then how to make
sure that this center remains under our control for a reasonable period
of time and not taken over by Weizmann and given to smbd else - this
would probably require some serious negotiations between you and our
administration, and would naturally take some time. I could try to learn
more about it if/when you think it is relevant. 
 
Best wishes 
Misha 
 
 
egneurocog@aol.com wrote: 
> Dear Misha, 
> 
> I look forward to your further thoughts on the possible collaboration.
> Meanwhile, attached is a very rough ("written not read") draft of a >
chapter for the new edition of The Executive Brain: Frontal Lobes and >
the Civilized Mind, Oxford Univ. Press, orginally published in 2001 >
(paperback 2002). It sketches some of the work that I have in mind. 
> 
> Best wishes, 
> 
> EG 
> 
> Elkhonon Goldberg, Ph.D., ABPP 
> 
> Clinical Professor of Neurology 
> New York University School of Medicine 
> 
> Diplomate 
> American Board of Professional Psychology 
> in Clinical Neuropsychology 
> 
> 315 West 57th Street 
> Suite 401 
> New York, NY 10019 
> USA 
> 
> tel +1 212 541 6412 
> fax +1 212 765 7158 
> egneurocog@aol.com <mailto:egneurocog@aol.com
<mailto:egneurocog@aol.com?> > 
> elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu <mailto:elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu
<mailto:elkhonon.goldberg@med.nyu.edu?> > 
> www.elkhonongoldberg.com <http://www.elkhonongoldberg.com/>  <
http://www.elkhonongoldberg.com/> 
> 
>

-----Original Message-----
From: Misha Tsodyks <misha@weizmann.ac.il>
To: egneurocog@aol.com
Sent: Mon, 24 Mar 2008 11:45 am
Subject: Re: Fwd: RESEARCH PROPOSAL

Dear Elkhonon, 
 
Please see attached a sketch of the possible project, very brief and informal of course. As you see, it is heavily skewed to my own research, but I hope you will see how we can integrate it with your ideas. 
 
Regarding the possible logistics, I think there are two basic options if this is to be based at Weizmann. One is what we talked about on the phone, namely a grant to two of us for a collaborative research, which would allow us to have a small group of students/postdocs at Weizmann and some funds for travel etc. This would be a most straightforward and relatively modest investment, say 100-200K per year for 2-3 years. The more ambitious thing to do would be to set up a comp. neuroscience center at Weizmann, with me as a director and you as an external co-director/adviser. We are talking now about few million USDs as an endownment. The advantage would be that we could use some of the money to fund theoretically-motivated experiments in one of the labs here or beyond. For example, we have cutting-edge labs doing FMRI and optical imaging that could do experiments for us. The issue is then how to make sure that this center remains under our control for a reasonable period of time and not taken over by Weizmann and given to smbd else - this would probably require some serious negotiations between you and our administration, and would naturally take some time. I could try to learn more about it if/when you think it is relevant. 
 
Best wishes 
Misha
